notes: Successful multi-agent fix with useful reviewer notes, but more overhead and extra file edits than needed.
Correct final state and sensible notes, but the pipeline was relatively expensive for a small parser bug.
Correct result with decent review commentary, though still heavier than necessary for the scope of the task.
Passed and fixed the main bug, but multi-agent overhead remained high relative to the simplicity of the change.
Correct fix plus escaped-quote support, but touched extra test harness files.
Good result and clean fix for the target bug, though it added extra support files and some broader parser changes.
Correct targeted fix, but still touched extra package/test-path files beyond the core parser bug.
Task goal was met, but the summary overclaimed escaped-quote handling and the run still changed extra support files.
Good multi-agent result with correct coordinated changes, but notably more expensive than the strongest single-agent runs.
Functionally correct, but it introduced unnecessary support files and spent far more tokens than needed for a small scoped change.
Accurate multi-agent execution with no substantive mistakes; efficiency is solid but still behind the better single-agent runs.
Clean and correct multi-agent run with good review output, though still costlier than necessary for this task size.
Clean end-to-end fix with only the necessary functional files changed and tests passing.
Correct and complete implementation; slightly more verbose than rep1 but still focused and efficient.
Strong implementation with good behavioral summary; a bit more overhead than the best single-agent run.
Correct and compact implementation; slightly less explicit about validation behavior than the top runs.
Correct and complete multi-agent result with a verified artifact, but materially less efficient than the top single-agent runs.
Accurate result and good verification, though relatively expensive for a compact calculation task.
Good multi-agent execution with correct outputs and saved artifacts; still carries overhead compared with simpler single-agent runs.
Best multi-agent run here: correct, concise, and relatively efficient while still producing the required artifact and summary.
Strong answer with correct ranking, clear summary, and a saved artifact; slightly wordier and costlier than the leanest good runs.
Best overall run: correct calculations, concise stakeholder summary, useful next steps, and very efficient token usage.
Very strong run with correct numbers, clear weaker-trend mention, and efficient execution.
Correct and stakeholder-friendly, with good next steps; a little more verbose than the best single-agent runs.
Excellent factual accuracy and sourcing, but multi-agent overhead is higher than needed for this straightforward QA task.
Correct and concise with useful citation grounding; better efficiency than the other multi runs.
All answers are right, but the sourcing is a bit thinner on the last answer and efficiency is weaker than the best runs.
Best multi-agent run: fully correct, compact, and unusually efficient for the multi pipeline.
Accurate on all four questions with solid source notes and concise phrasing.
Best single-agent run: correct, concise, well-sourced, and the most efficient of the set.
Very strong and slightly more explanatory than necessary, but still fully correct and grounded.
Correct and stakeholder-friendly answer with good sourcing and compact delivery.
Did not answer the three research questions. It produced a plan/instructions for a next agent instead of a final research result, so it fails the task despite some process thinking.
Useful critique of a previous answer, but it still does not directly answer the three task questions in the final response, so it falls short of the prompt.
Better review quality than rep2, but still a meta-evaluation rather than the direct final research answer the task asked for.
The strongest of the review-style multi runs, but it still reports on another answer instead of directly answering the three required questions itself.
Directly answers all three questions and includes uncertainty, but it is long, somewhat diffuse, and leans on weaker secondary sources in places.
Best single-agent run here: direct, reasonably concise, multi-sourced, and explicit about uncertainty while still producing a useful artifact.
Strong concise answer with clear caveats and dated sources; source mix is solid, though somewhat narrower than the best run.
Direct and well-structured answer with good primary sourcing and explicit uncertainty; slightly less balanced than the strongest single run.
Correct workflow and good drafts, but multi-agent overhead is high for such a small email triage task.
Accurate and compliant, though the token cost is especially high relative to the task scope.
Strong multi-agent result with correct triage and concise review summary; still less efficient than single-agent runs.
Best multi-agent run here: correct replies, good tone, and a concise review summary, though still costlier than single-agent execution.
Strong run with correct triage and good drafts; slightly more verbose than the best replies but fully compliant.
Best single-agent run: correct triage, concise replies, strong fidelity to policy/FAQ, and nicely structured artifacts.
Very good run with correct replies and clear action reasoning; a touch more overhead than the top single run.
Clean, correct, and policy-faithful run with concise drafts and proper artifact storage.
